sourceMovie.title: The Outsiders
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Outsiders (1983) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gone Baby Gone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Outsiders clicked for you, Gone Baby Gone (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Lawless
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Outsiders clicked for you, Lawless (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Outsiders?
faq.answer: Try Gone Baby Gone, Lawless, Boyz n the Hood—each shares DNA with The Outsiders while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Outsiders?
faq.answer: The Outsiders (1983) is best described as Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Outsiders worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
